title: dactylorhiza cordigera subsp. graeca
intro: Dactylorhiza cordigera subsp. graeca is a subspecies of orchid native to Greece, characterized by its unique leaf structure and vibrant purple flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza cordigera subsp. graeca include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the roots in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried flowers in a decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
